policy.title: RBI finalizes Climate Risk Management Guidelines for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines mandating SCBs to conduct climate stress tests by March 2027, with physical risk assessments required for exposures exceeding ₹100 crore. Banks must report sector-wise transition risk metrics quarterly starting October 2026.

policy.title: IRDAI releases draft guidelines on Index-Based Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI's draft framework permits parametric products triggered by weather indices (rainfall, temperature, wind speed) with automatic payouts within 15 days. Insurers must use ISRO or IMD-certified data sources for trigger validation.

policy.title: SEBI extends BRSR Core to top 500 listed companies, adds Scope 3 requirements
policy.description: SEBI mandates BRSR Core reporting for top 500 companies by market cap from FY 2026-27, requiring limited assurance on Scope 1, 2 and material Scope 3 emissions. Value chain ESG assessments now mandatory for top 250 companies.

policy.title: Basel Committee publishes consultation on climate risk capital add-ons under Pillar 2
policy.description: BCBS proposes supervisory guidance allowing regulators to impose capital add-ons of 0-3% for banks with concentrated exposures to high physical risk sectors. Consultation closes June 2026 with implementation expected by 2028.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk variables
policy.description: Updated scenarios incorporate compound climate events and regional granularity for South Asia, with GDP impact projections for India ranging from -8% to -24% by 2050 under different warming pathways. New acute hazard probability distributions included.

policy.title: European Commission confirms CSRD applicability for non-EU companies from 2028
policy.description: Non-EU companies with €150M+ EU revenue must report under ESRS starting FY 2028. Indian IT services and pharma exporters with significant EU presence will need climate transition plans aligned with Paris Agreement goals.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for India physical risk analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter signed a multi-year deal with HDFC Bank to provide asset-level flood and cyclone risk scoring for the bank's ₹18 lakh crore loan book. Implementation begins Q2 2026 covering commercial real estate and infrastructure portfolios.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory raises $12M Series B led by Omnivore Partners
competitors.description: Funding will expand GIST's natural capital accounting platform and build climate risk modules for Indian banks. Company claims 45 enterprise clients including three PSU banks piloting their biodiversity risk assessment tools.

competitors.title: Moody's launches India-specific Physical Climate Risk Score for banks
competitors.description: New product covers 650+ Indian districts with hazard scores for floods, cyclones, heat stress, and water scarcity at PIN code level. Integrates with Moody's credit risk models for automated PD adjustments.

competitors.title: CarbonMint selected by SBI for agricultural carbon credit verification
competitors.description: State Bank of India will use CarbonMint's blockchain-based MRV platform to verify carbon credits from 50,000 farmer borrowers under its sustainable agriculture lending program. Pilot covers rice and cotton cultivation in Maharashtra.

competitors.title: XDI releases Gross Asset Damage scores for 2.5 million Indian properties
competitors.description: Australia-based XDI expanded its physical risk database to cover Indian commercial and industrial properties, projecting asset damage under RCP 4.5 and 8.5 scenarios through 2100. Data available via API for portfolio screening.

competitors.title: Watershed raises $150M Series D, announces emerging markets expansion
competitors.description: Valuation reaches $2.8B with new funding from Greenoaks Capital. Company announced plans to open India and Brazil offices by Q4 2026, targeting financial services clients with localized emissions factors and regulatory reporting templates.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: Sectoral Stress Testing Methodology
research.description: RBI's technical paper proposes standardized stress testing approaches showing agriculture NPAs could rise 180-340 bps under severe drought scenarios. Recommends banks incorporate district-level hazard data into internal credit risk models.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Lessons from Catastrophe Bond Markets
research.description: Report finds parametric products could close 60% of India's $15B annual protection gap for climate perils. Highlights correlation basis risk as key barrier, recommending hybrid index designs combining satellite and ground station data.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Capital Flows
research.description: IFC analysis of 12,000 emerging market companies finds only 23% have adequate climate disclosures versus 67% in developed markets. Data gaps cost emerging market issuers an estimated 40-80 bps in higher borrowing costs.

research.title: Climate-Adjusted Probability of Default: A Framework for Asian Banks
research.description: UNEPFI working group presents methodology for adjusting corporate PDs based on physical and transition risk exposure. Pilot with 8 Asian banks showed climate-adjusted PDs 15-45% higher for carbon-intensive sectors by 2035.

research.title: Flood Risk Financing in India: The Case for Sovereign Parametric Solutions
research.description: Study recommends India establish a national parametric flood insurance facility with $500M-1B coverage, triggered by river gauge and satellite precipitation data. Estimates fiscal savings of ₹8,000-12,000 crore over 10 years versus post-disaster spending.

research.title: Building Climate-Resilient Financial Systems in Emerging Economies
research.description: BIS paper highlights that emerging market banks hold 3-5x higher physical risk exposure than developed market peers. Calls for enhanced supervisory capacity and standardized climate risk taxonomies adapted to regional hazard profiles.

ourRead.headline: India's regulatory apparatus is crystallizing around climate risk mandates—StepChange has 18 months to become the default infrastructure before global players fully localize.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: the Indian financial system's climate risk architecture is being built right now, with deadlines hardening and capital beginning to move. RBI's finalized guidelines, IRDAI's parametric framework, and SEBI's BRSR expansion represent coordinated regulatory pressure that will force every scheduled commercial bank to procure climate risk analytics by late 2026. The question is no longer whether banks will buy these capabilities, but from whom.

The RBI's March 28 guidelines are the most consequential policy signal. The ₹100 crore exposure threshold for physical risk assessments means mid-sized commercial and infrastructure loans are now in scope—exactly the segment where StepChange's district-level hazard data creates the most value. The October 2026 quarterly reporting deadline is aggressive; banks will need vendors operational by Q2. This is our window. The Basel Pillar 2 consultation on capital add-ons (0-3%) also signals that climate risk will soon directly impact bank capital adequacy—CFOs will start caring about this, not just sustainability teams.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership is the competitive signal that demands response. A global climate risk leader has now secured India's largest private bank, validating our thesis but narrowing our runway. However, Moody's PIN code-level product and XDI's 2.5 million property database reveal the strategy of global players: breadth over depth, global models localized rather than India-native solutions. Our differentiation must be precision—integrating IMD ground truth, state disaster management data, and actual claims history that these platforms cannot easily acquire. The GIST Advisory fundraise shows Indian capital is also flowing to local players; we should view them as potential partners for ESG adjacencies rather than direct threats.

The RBI's technical paper on stress testing methodology is a gift—it essentially provides the specification sheet for what banks must build. The 180-340 bps NPA increase projections for agriculture under drought scenarios give us concrete numbers to reference in sales conversations. Combined with UNEPFI's climate-adjusted PD framework showing 15-45% higher defaults in carbon-intensive sectors, we now have authoritative backing for our core value proposition. The Swiss Re parametric report identifying correlation basis risk as the key barrier directly supports our hybrid index design work; we should cite this in IRDAI consultations.

One thing to watch: Watershed's India office announcement. A $2.8B player entering our market with dedicated resources changes the competitive landscape for ESG platform deals, even if they're initially focused on enterprise carbon accounting rather than bank credit risk. One action for this week: draft a response to the IRDAI parametric guidelines consultation emphasizing the need for IMD/ISRO data standardization—positioning StepChange as a technical thought leader before the framework finalizes.
